chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ النَّظَرُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ مَوْلَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 167 – What is Permissible for the slaves to look at from his mistress

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/368)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/368)
text: لَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ يَنْظُرَ عَبْدُهَا إِلَى شَيْ‏ءٍ مِنْ جَسَدِهَا إِلَّا إِلَى شَعْرِهَا غَيْرَ مُتَعَمِّدٍ لِذَلِكَ
وَ فِي رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَنْظُرَ إِلَى شَعْرِهَا إِذَا كَانَ مَأْمُوناً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Yunus Bin Ammar and Yunus Bin Yaqoub, altogether,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is not Permissible for the woman that her slave should see anything from her body except for her hair, without deliberation to that’.
And in another report, ‘(He<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘There is no problem if he looks at her hair when he was a Believer’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Mahbub from Yunus ibn ‘Ammar and Yunus ibn Ya’qub all of them have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not lawful for a slave to look at any part of the body of his mistress except her hairs unintentionally.’” In another Hadith it is said that it is not unlawful to look at her hairs if it is safe (from sinful feelings).
